Thursday, April 23, 2026 Daily Digest

Google Cloud NEXT: AI Agents Are the Enterprise Play. Adobe CX Enterprise Goes GA. WPP Bridges Digital and Physical.

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

Google Puts AI Agents at Heart of Enterprise Push

At Google Cloud NEXT 2026 in Las Vegas, Alphabet signaled that AI agents — human-like digital assistants — are the linchpin of its enterprise money-making strategy. The message to investors is clear: Google is going all-in on agentic AI as the next wave of enterprise software. This is Google telling the market where the puck is going.

Read on Reuters →
2

Adobe CX Enterprise: The Agentic Marketing OS

Adobe Summit 2026 delivered a big announcement: Adobe CX Enterprise — a new end-to-end system unifying AI agents, reusable skills, MCP connection points, and governance layers. The platform coordinates data, content, decisions, and execution through orchestrated agents. With 20,000+ global brands already on Adobe technology, this isn't a moonshot — it's infrastructure.

Read on Cloud News →
3

WPP Integrates Google Earth AI for Hyper-Local Campaigns

WPP is now one of the first marketing companies to integrate Google's Earth AI — planetary-scale geospatial intelligence — into WPP Open, its agentic marketing platform. The use case: connect digital behavior with physical-world signals (traffic, weather, movement patterns) to predict demand and automate hyper-local campaigns. One automotive client saw 77% higher performance using an "EV Readiness Index" built on this data.

Read on PRNewswire →
4

Adobe's $25B Buyback Signals AI Agent Confidence

Alongside CX Enterprise, Adobe's board authorized a massive $25 billion share repurchase program running through 2030. The message: Adobe is betting big on its agentic AI portfolio across CX Enterprise, GenStudio, Firefly, and Experience Manager. Capital allocation is strategy made visible.

Read on Yahoo Finance →
5

OpenAI Expands Codex Partnerships with Consulting Giants

OpenAI is expanding partnerships with major global consulting firms to accelerate enterprise adoption of its Codex AI tools. As competition in the AI market intensifies, OpenAI is leaning on consultancies to help enterprises actually implement — not just experiment with — AI at scale. The shift from "AI awareness" to "AI deployment" is the enterprise challenge of 2026.

Read on Reuters →

💡 My Take

This week is Google Cloud NEXT week, and the theme is unmistakable: AI agents are enterprise infrastructure now. Google, Adobe, and WPP are all positioning agentic AI as the orchestration layer for business — not just a tool, but the operating system. The WPP + Earth AI story is particularly interesting: bridging digital signals with physical-world data is exactly the kind of differentiation that will separate winners from also-rans in the enterprise AI race.
